title: Multimedia Studio - Standalone React Application with MP3 Cutter, Video Cutter, Video Downloader, and IP Camera

requirements.summary: Create standalone Multimedia App with tabbed navigation, dark/light mode toggle, and mobile-responsive layout
acceptanceCriteria: App renders with four clearly labeled tabs: MP3 Cutter, Video Cutter, Video Downloader, IP Camera
Dark/light mode toggle is present in the header and switches the theme
Tab switching is smooth with animation
Layout is fully mobile-responsive on screens 320px and wider
Active tab is visually highlighted

file_operations.description: Create the root component for the standalone Multimedia Studio application. Implement a tabbed navigation layout with four tabs (MP3 Cutter, Video Cutter, Video Downloader, IP Camera) using state management to track the active tab. Include a header with dark/light mode toggle using the same theme context pattern from the existing App.tsx. Apply smooth tab-switching animations using CSS transitions. Ensure the layout is fully mobile-responsive with a horizontal scrollable tab bar on smaller screens. Use the glassmorphism design pattern with backdrop-filter blur and semi-transparent card surfaces from frontend/src/index.css utility classes.

Create a header component for the Multimedia App featuring the app title 'Multimedia Studio', a dark/light theme toggle button (reusing the theme logic from frontend/src/components/Header.tsx), and responsive layout. Apply the deep charcoal/slate background with glassmorphism styling and teal/cyan accent colors for the theme toggle.

Create a tab navigation component that renders four tabs: 'MP3 Cutter', 'Video Cutter', 'Video Downloader', and 'IP Camera'. Implement active tab highlighting with teal/cyan accent color. Add smooth transition animations on tab switching. Make the tab bar horizontally scrollable on mobile screens (320px+). Each tab should have an icon (use lucide-react icons) and label.

Wire the new MultimediaApp component into the application routing. Import and conditionally render MultimediaApp alongside the existing App component based on a route or query parameter (e.g., render MultimediaApp when pathname is '/multimedia' or render a navigation link to switch between apps).

requirements.summary: Implement MP3 Cutter module with waveform visualization, draggable trim handles, and client-side audio cutting using Web Audio API
acceptanceCriteria: File picker accepts .mp3, .aac, .wav, .mp4 files
Waveform is rendered on a Canvas after file is loaded
Two draggable handles allow selecting start and end positions on the waveform
Selected start time and end time are displayed numerically in seconds or MM:SS format
Clicking 'Cut & Download' triggers a browser download of the trimmed audio as an MP3 file
The downloaded file contains only the audio between the selected start and end markers

file_operations.description: Create the main MP3 Cutter module component. Provide a file upload button accepting .mp3, .aac, .wav, .mp4 files. After upload, use the Web Audio API to decode the audio file into an AudioBuffer. Render a waveform visualization on a Canvas element by extracting PCM data from the AudioBuffer and drawing amplitude peaks. Display the total audio duration. Implement two draggable range handles (start and end markers) overlaid on the waveform canvas to define the trim region. Show the selected start and end times in MM:SS format. Provide a 'Cut & Download' button that extracts the selected audio segment by slicing the AudioBuffer, encodes it to MP3 using lamejs (loaded lazily from CDN: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/lamejs@1.2.0/lame.min.js), and triggers a browser download. Include a loading spinner during encoding. Apply the glassmorphism card styling and teal/cyan accent buttons.

Create a custom hook managing MP3 cutter state and logic. Handle file loading and decoding via Web Audio API. Manage waveform data extraction (downsample PCM data for canvas rendering). Track start/end trim positions and convert them to time values. Implement audio slicing logic by creating a new AudioBuffer from the selected range. Lazy-load lamejs encoder from CDN and encode the AudioBuffer slice to MP3 format. Expose functions for file upload, trim position updates, and audio export.

Create a reusable waveform canvas component that renders audio amplitude data as a visual waveform. Accept PCM data, canvas dimensions, and current trim positions (start/end) as props. Draw the waveform peaks with a teal/cyan gradient. Render two draggable trim handle overlays (vertical lines with grab handles) that users can drag to adjust start and end positions. Implement mouse and touch event handlers for dragging. Highlight the selected region between the handles with a semi-transparent teal overlay.
Wire the MP3Cutter module into the tabbed navigation. Import frontend/src/modules/MP3Cutter.tsx and render it when the 'MP3 Cutter' tab is active. Pass any necessary props or callbacks.

requirements.summary: Implement Video Cutter module with video preview, timeline scrubber with draggable trim handles, and client-side video cutting using ffmpeg.wasm
acceptanceCriteria: File picker accepts MP4, WebM, MOV, AVI, and MKV files
Uploaded video is shown in a playback preview element
Timeline scrubber with two draggable trim handles is displayed below the video
Start and end time values update in real time as handles are dragged
Clicking 'Cut' processes the video and downloads the trimmed segment
A progress/loading indicator is shown during processing
Downloaded file contains only the selected clip

file_operations.description: Create the main Video Cutter module component. Provide a file upload button accepting MP4, WebM, MOV, AVI, MKV files. After upload, display the video in an HTML5 <video> element for playback preview. Render a timeline scrubber UI below the video with two draggable trim handles (start and end points) that users can drag along the timeline to define the clip region. Show the selected start and end times numerically in MM:SS format, updating in real time as handles are dragged. Provide a 'Cut & Download' button that uses ffmpeg.wasm (lazy-loaded from CDN: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@ffmpeg/ffmpeg@0.12.10/dist/umd/ffmpeg.js and https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@ffmpeg/util@0.12.1/dist/umd/index.js) to trim the video and download the result. Display a loading spinner with progress percentage during processing. Apply glassmorphism styling and teal/cyan accent buttons.

Create a custom hook managing video cutter state and logic. Handle video file loading and metadata extraction (duration). Track start/end trim positions in seconds. Lazy-load ffmpeg.wasm from CDN and initialize the FFmpeg instance. Implement video trimming by loading the video file into ffmpeg.wasm, running the trim command with start/end timestamps, and extracting the output video blob. Expose functions for file upload, trim position updates, progress tracking, and video export.

Create a reusable video timeline scrubber component. Accept video duration, current trim positions (start/end), and update callbacks as props. Render a horizontal timeline bar with tick marks representing time intervals. Overlay two draggable trim handles (vertical bars with grab icons) that users can drag to adjust start and end positions. Implement mouse and touch event handlers for dragging. Highlight the selected clip region between the handles with a teal/cyan semi-transparent overlay. Display time labels at the handle positions.
Wire the VideoCutter module into the tabbed navigation. Import frontend/src/modules/VideoCutter.tsx and render it when the 'Video Cutter' tab is active.

requirements.summary: Implement Video Downloader module that fetches YouTube video metadata via YouTube Data API v3 and opens the video in a new tab with user guidance
acceptanceCriteria: URL input field accepts a pasted YouTube link
Clicking 'Download' fetches metadata from YouTube Data API v3 using the provided API key
Video thumbnail and title are displayed after metadata is fetched
Download button opens the YouTube URL in a new tab
A clear message informs the user the video will open in a new tab for saving
Loading spinner is shown while fetching metadata
A clear error message is displayed if the URL is invalid or the API call fails

file_operations.description: Modify the existing VideoDownloader component (frontend/src/modules/VideoDownloader.tsx) to implement the full requirement. Update the component to use the YouTube Data API v3 with the provided API key 'AIzaSyDlj5yvdIpE6OYJnIw_oJg5Cz5_kRJ33YQ'. After fetching metadata, display the video thumbnail and title in a card with glassmorphism styling. Replace or enhance the download button to open the YouTube watch URL in a new browser tab using window.open(url, '_blank'). Display a clear, user-facing message: 'The video will open in a new tab — use your browser or a download extension to save it.' Show a loading spinner (lucide-react Loader2 icon with spin animation) while fetching metadata. Display user-friendly error messages if the URL is invalid or the API call fails (e.g., 'Invalid YouTube URL' or 'Failed to fetch video info'). Apply teal/cyan accent styling to the download button.
Wire the VideoDownloader module into the tabbed navigation. Import frontend/src/modules/VideoDownloader.tsx and render it when the 'Video Downloader' tab is active.

requirements.summary: Implement IP Camera module that streams live video from the device camera and displays the local WiFi IP address via WebRTC ICE candidate gathering
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'IP Cam' button requests camera permission and starts the live video stream in the panel
Live video feed is displayed in a <video> element
The device's local WiFi IP address is detected and displayed after the camera starts
The displayed address is in the format 'http://<local-ip>' so the user can share it
A note explains that devices on the same network can use this address to view the stream
A 'Stop Camera' button stops the stream and clears the video feed
A clear error message is shown if camera permission is denied

file_operations.description: Create the IP Camera module component. Use the camera component's useCamera hook (imported from 'camera/useCamera') to manage camera access and streaming. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing. Provide an 'IP Cam' button that calls startCamera() to request camera access and start the live video stream. Display the live video feed in a <video> element using the videoRef from useCamera. Ensure the video preview has explicit, non-zero dimensions (e.g., width: 100%, aspect-ratio: 16/9, or min-height: 300px) so it never collapses. After the camera starts (isActive becomes true), detect the device's local WiFi IP address using WebRTC ICE candidate gathering: create an RTCPeerConnection with a public STUN server (stun:stun.l.google.com:19302), create a dummy data channel, set a local description, and extract the local IP from the ICE candidates. Display the detected IP address in the format 'Access camera at: http://<local-ip>'. Include a note: 'Devices on the same WiFi network can use this address to view the stream.' Provide a 'Stop Camera' button that calls stopCamera() to stop the stream and release the camera. Display a clear error message if camera permission is denied (check the error prop from useCamera). Do not make camera buttons clickable until the camera is fully initialized (disable buttons while isLoading). Apply glassmorphism card styling and teal/cyan accent buttons. On desktop, ensure camera cannot be switched (only 'environment' facing mode is working). Always show the camera preview when the user opens the camera. Properly display camera error messages in the app.

Create a custom hook for detecting the local WiFi IP address via WebRTC ICE candidate gathering. Implement the logic: create an RTCPeerConnection with a STUN server configuration (stun:stun.l.google.com:19302), create a dummy data channel to trigger ICE gathering, set a local SDP offer, listen for ICE candidates, and parse the candidate strings to extract the local IP address (look for candidates with type 'host'). Return the detected IP address or null if detection fails. Handle cleanup by closing the RTCPeerConnection after detection.
Wire the IPCamera module into the tabbed navigation. Import frontend/src/modules/IPCamera.tsx and render it when the 'IP Camera' tab is active.

requirements.summary: Apply cohesive dark-first visual design system with deep charcoal/slate backgrounds, teal/cyan accents, glassmorphism, and smooth animations across all four modules
acceptanceCriteria: All four module tabs use deep charcoal/slate backgrounds
Teal/cyan accent colors are applied to primary buttons and active tab indicators
Card surfaces use glassmorphism styling (backdrop blur, semi-transparent background)
Typography is crisp white on dark backgrounds
Button hover states and tab transitions have smooth animations
Design is consistent across all four modules

file_operations.description: Extend the existing global stylesheet (frontend/src/index.css) to include additional utility classes for the Multimedia App's design system. Ensure deep charcoal/slate background colors are available (use existing CSS custom properties like --background or define new ones if needed for darker shades). Define teal/cyan accent color utilities (e.g., .accent-teal with background: oklch(0.65 0.2 195)) for buttons and active states. Ensure glassmorphism utility classes like .glass-card are available (backdrop-filter: blur(12px), background: rgba(255,255,255,0.1), border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2)). Add smooth transition utilities for hover states and animations (e.g., transition: all 0.3s ease). Ensure crisp white typography is the default on dark backgrounds (color: oklch(1 0 0)).

Apply the dark-first design system to the MultimediaApp root component. Set a deep charcoal/slate background color on the main container (e.g., bg-slate-900 or a custom OKLCH dark background). Ensure all child components (header, tabs, module panels) inherit the theme. Apply glassmorphism styling to the main content area container using the .glass-card utility class. Ensure smooth transitions for tab switching (e.g., fade-in/fade-out or slide animations using CSS transitions or framer-motion if already available).

Apply the dark-first design to the MultimediaHeader component. Use deep charcoal/slate background with glassmorphism (backdrop-filter blur and semi-transparent background). Apply teal/cyan accent color to the theme toggle button. Ensure crisp white typography for the app title. Add smooth hover animations to the theme toggle button (scale or glow effect on hover).

Apply the design system to the MultimediaTabNavigation component. Use deep charcoal/slate background for the tab bar. Apply teal/cyan accent color to the active tab indicator (border-bottom or background highlight). Add smooth transition animations when switching tabs (e.g., sliding underline or fade-in effect). Ensure tab labels use crisp white typography. Add hover effects to inactive tabs (subtle brightness increase or scale on hover).

Apply the design system to the MP3Cutter module. Use glassmorphism card styling for the main content container. Apply teal/cyan accent color to primary action buttons ('Upload', 'Cut & Download'). Ensure all typography is crisp white. Add smooth hover animations to buttons (scale or glow effect). Use deep charcoal/slate background for the waveform canvas container.

Apply the design system to the VideoCutter module. Use glassmorphism card styling for the main content container. Apply teal/cyan accent color to primary action buttons ('Upload', 'Cut & Download'). Ensure all typography is crisp white. Add smooth hover animations to buttons. Use deep charcoal/slate background for the video player and timeline container.

Apply the design system to the VideoDownloader module. Use glassmorphism card styling for the video info display card. Apply teal/cyan accent color to the 'Download' button. Ensure all typography (title, instructions, error messages) is crisp white. Add smooth hover animations to buttons.

Apply the design system to the IPCamera module. Use glassmorphism card styling for the main content container. Apply teal/cyan accent color to primary action buttons ('IP Cam', 'Stop Camera'). Ensure all typography (IP address display, instructions, error messages) is crisp white. Add smooth hover animations to buttons. Use deep charcoal/slate background for the video preview container.
